content:
# Exodus Chapter 33 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 103–104; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 76–77 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_033_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_033_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_033_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_033_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — FACE TO FACE WITH YHWH:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 33 across pages 103–104, continuing directly from the aftermath of the golden calf. This chapter pivots from judgment to radical intimacy: YHWH speaks with Moses face to face (panim el-panim), yet declares that no man can see his face and live. The theological tension between divine presence and divine hiddenness is the axis of the entire chapter. The Vaticanus text (pp. 76–77) largely follows MT but expands Moses' dialogue at several points.

---

## The Command to Depart and the Threatened Withdrawal (vv. 1–6)

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, "Go [lekh], go up [aleh] from here, you and the people whom you brought up [he'eleita] from the land of Egypt, to the land which I swore [nishbati] to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, saying, 'To your seed [le-zar'akha] I will give it.'"

> **Key terms:** הֶעֱלֵיתָ (he'eleita) = "you caused to go up" — Hiphil; YHWH still attributes the exodus to Moses, maintaining the distance of ch. 32. **LXX:** ἀνάβηθι ("go up"), matching MT.

**2** "And I will send [ve-shalakhti] before you an angel [mal'akh], and I will drive out [ve-gerashti] the Canaanite, the Amorite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, the Hivite, and the Jebusite —"

> **Key terms:** מַלְאָךְ (mal'akh) = "messenger/angel" — a mediated presence, not YHWH himself. **LXX:** τὸν ἄγγελόν μου ("my angel"), adding the possessive pronoun.

**3** "— to a land flowing [zavat] with milk [khalav] and honey [u-devash]; for I will not go up [lo e'eleh] in your midst [be-qirbekha], for you are a stiff-necked [qesheh-oref] people, lest I consume you [akhelkha] on the way [ba-derekh]."

> **Key terms:** בְּקִרְבְּךָ (be-qirbekha) = "in your inner-part/midst" — from קֶרֶב (qerev), denoting intimate interior presence. The threat of absence is the crisis of the chapter. **LXX:** ἐν σοί ("among you"), less spatially vivid.

**4** And the people heard [va-yishma] this evil word [ha-davar ha-ra ha-zeh], and they mourned [va-yit'abbalu]; and no man put [ve-lo shatu ish] his ornaments [edyo] upon him.

> **Key terms:** וַיִּתְאַבְּלוּ (va-yit'abbalu) = "they mourned" — Hithpael of אבל, reflexive mourning. עֶדְיוֹ (edyo) = "his ornaments/adornments."

**5** And YHWH said to Moses, "Say to the Sons of Israel, 'You are a stiff-necked [qesheh-oref] people; one moment [rega ekhad] I could go up [e'eleh] in your midst [be-qirbekha] and I would consume you [ve-khillitikha]. And now, put down [hored] your ornaments [edyekha] from upon you, and I will know [ve-ed'ah] what I will do [mah e'eseh] to you.'"

> **Key terms:** רֶגַע אֶחָד (rega ekhad) = "one moment/instant" — the fragility of Israel's existence before a holy God. וְכִלִּיתִיךָ (ve-khillitikha) = "and I would finish/consume you" — Piel of כלה. **LXX:** ἐξολεθρεύσω ὑμᾶς ("I would utterly destroy you").

**6** And the Sons of Israel stripped [va-yitnatslu] their ornaments [et-edyam] from Mount Horeb [me-har Khorev] onward.

> **Key terms:** וַיִּתְנַצְּלוּ (va-yitnatslu) = "they stripped off from themselves" — Hithpael of נצל. מֵהַר חֹרֵב (me-har Khorev) = "from Mount Horeb" — the stripping is permanent, beginning at the mountain of revelation. **LXX:** τὴν περιστολὴν αὐτῶν ("their apparel"), broadening beyond ornaments.

---

## The Tent of Meeting Outside the Camp (vv. 7–11)

**7** And Moses would take [yiqqakh] the tent [ha-ohel] and pitch it [ve-natah lo] outside [mi-khuts] the camp [la-makhaneh], far off [harkhek] from the camp, and he called it [ve-qara lo] the Tent of Meeting [Ohel Mo'ed]; and it was, everyone who sought [kol mevaqesh] YHWH would go out [yetse] to the Tent of Meeting which was outside the camp.

> **Key terms:** אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד (Ohel Mo'ed) = "Tent of Appointed-Meeting" — from יעד ("to appoint/meet"). Its placement outside the camp enacts YHWH's threatened withdrawal. **LXX:** τὴν σκηνὴν αὐτοῦ ("his tent"), then ἡ σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου ("tent of testimony") — a different theological framing.

**8** And it was, when Moses went out [be-tset Moshe] to the tent, all the people would rise [yaqumu] and stand [ve-nitsvu], each man at the opening of his tent [petakh oholo], and they would gaze [ve-hibitu] after Moses until he entered [bo'o] the tent.

> **Key terms:** וְהִבִּיטוּ (ve-hibitu) = "and they gazed" — Hiphil of נבט; an intense, fixed gaze. The people watch Moses as the sole mediator.

**9** And it was, when Moses entered [ke-vo] the tent, the pillar of cloud [ammud he-anan] would descend [yered] and stand [ve-amad] at the opening [petakh] of the tent, and he would speak [ve-dibber] with Moses.

> **Key terms:** עַמּוּד הֶעָנָן (ammud he-anan) = "pillar of the cloud" — the visible sign of YHWH's presence descending to the tent entrance. **LXX:** ὁ στῦλος τῆς νεφέλης, exact equivalent.

**10** And all the people would see [ve-ra'ah] the pillar of cloud standing [omed] at the opening of the tent, and all the people would rise [ve-qam] and bow down [ve-hishtakhavu], each man at the opening of his tent.

> **Key terms:** וְהִשְׁתַּחֲווּ (ve-hishtakhavu) = "and they bowed down/prostrated" — Hishtaphel of חוה; worship from a distance, each at his own tent door.

**11** And YHWH would speak [ve-dibber] to Moses face to face [panim el-panim], as a man [ish] speaks [yedabber] to his companion [el-re'ehu]; and he would return [ve-shav] to the camp, and his attendant [mesharto] Joshua [Yehoshua] son of Nun, a young man [na'ar], would not depart [lo yamish] from within [mi-tokh] the tent.

> **Key terms:** פָּנִים אֶל־פָּנִים (panim el-panim) = "face to face" — the supreme expression of unmediated intimacy, standing in tension with v. 20 ("you cannot see my face"). מְשָׁרְתוֹ (mesharto) = "his attendant/minister" — Piel participle of שׁרת. **LXX:** ἐνώπιος ἐνωπίῳ ("face-to-face"), an exact calque.

---

## Moses' Plea: "Show Me Your Ways" (vv. 12–17)

**12** And Moses said to YHWH, "See [re'eh], you are saying [omer] to me, 'Bring up [ha'al] this people,' and you — you have not made known to me [lo hodatani] whom [et asher] you will send [tishlakh] with me; and you, you said [amarta], 'I know you [yedatikha] by name [be-shem],' and also, 'You have found [matsata] favor [khen] in my eyes [be-einai].'"

> **Key terms:** יְדַעְתִּיךָ בְשֵׁם (yedatikha be-shem) = "I have known you by name" — personal, covenantal knowing. חֵן (khen) = "grace/favor" — the hinge-word of Moses' entire argument.

**13** "And now, if indeed [im-na] I have found favor in your eyes, make known to me [hodi'eni na] your ways [et-derakhekha], and I will know you [ve-eda'akha], so that [lema'an] I may find [emtsa] favor [khen] in your eyes; and consider [u-re'eh] that this nation [ha-goy ha-zeh] is your people [ammekha]."

> **Key terms:** דְּרָכֶיךָ (derakhekha) = "your ways" — Moses asks not for a vision but for knowledge of YHWH's character and governance. **LXX:** adds ἐμφάνισόν μοι σεαυτόν ("reveal yourself to me"), an expansion not in MT.

**14** And he said, "My face [panai] will go [yelkhu], and I will give rest [va-hanikhoti] to you."

> **Key terms:** פָּנַי יֵלֵכוּ (panai yelkhu) = "my face/presence will go" — פָּנִים here functions as YHWH's own personal presence. וַהֲנִחֹתִי (va-hanikhoti) = "and I will give you rest" — Hiphil of נוח. **LXX:** αὐτός σε προπορεύσομαι ("I myself will go before you"), interpreting "face" as YHWH himself.

**15** And he said to him, "If your face [panekha] is not going [ein holkhim], do not bring us up [al ta'alenu] from here."

> **Key terms:** אֵין (ein) = "there is not" — Moses makes YHWH's personal presence the non-negotiable condition of the journey.

**16** "And in what [u-va-meh] will it be known [yivvada] then [efo] that I have found favor in your eyes — I and your people? Is it not in your going [be-lekhtekha] with us [immanu], and we will be distinguished [ve-nifleinu] — I and your people — from every people [mi-kol ha-am] that is on the face of the ground [al-pene ha-adamah]?"

> **Key terms:** וְנִפְלֵינוּ (ve-nifleinu) = "and we shall be made distinct/separated" — Niphal of פלא; the same root as "wonder/miracle." Israel's distinction from the nations depends entirely on YHWH's accompanying presence. **LXX:** ἐνδοξασθησόμεθα ("we shall be glorified"), a different theological nuance.

**17** And YHWH said to Moses, "Also [gam] this thing [ha-davar ha-zeh] that you have spoken [dibbarta] I will do [e'eseh], for you have found favor [khen] in my eyes, and I know you [va-eda'akha] by name [be-shem]."

> **Key terms:** The double grounding — favor (khen) and knowing by name — confirms YHWH's relenting. Moses has succeeded.

---

## Moses' Supreme Request: "Show Me Your Glory" (vv. 18–23)

**18** And he said, "Show me [har'eni na], I pray, your glory [et-kevodekha]."

> **Key terms:** כְּבֹדֶךָ (kevodekha) = "your glory/weight/heaviness" — from כבד (kavod, "to be heavy"); Moses escalates from "ways" (v. 13) to the unveiled radiance of YHWH himself. **LXX:** τὴν σεαυτοῦ δόξαν ("your own glory").

**19** And he said, "I myself will cause all my goodness [kol-tuvi] to pass [a'avir] before your face [al-panekha], and I will proclaim [ve-qarati] the name of YHWH [be-shem YHWH] before you [lefanekha]; and I will be gracious [ve-khannoti] to whom I will be gracious [et-asher akhon], and I will have compassion [ve-rikhamti] on whom I will have compassion [et-asher arakhem]."

> **Key terms:** טוּבִי (tuvi) = "my goodness" — YHWH's glory is revealed as his moral goodness. וְחַנֹּתִי אֶת־אֲשֶׁר אָחֹן (ve-khannoti et-asher akhon) = "and I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious" — sovereign freedom in mercy expressed through the idem-per-idem construction. **LXX:** ἐλεήσω ὃν ἂν ἐλεῶ ("I will have mercy on whom I have mercy"), preserving the construction.

**20** And he said, "You are not able [lo tukhal] to see [lir'ot] my face [et-panai], for no man [ha-adam] shall see me [yir'ani] and live [va-khai]."

> **Key terms:** לֹא־יִרְאַנִי הָאָדָם וָחָי (lo-yir'ani ha-adam va-khai) = "the man shall not see me and live" — the absolute limit of creaturely encounter with divine holiness. The tension with v. 11 ("face to face") is deliberate and irreducible.

**21** And YHWH said, "Behold [hinneh], there is a place [maqom] beside me [itti], and you shall stand [ve-nitsavta] upon the rock [al-ha-tsur]."

> **Key terms:** מָקוֹם (maqom) = "place" — YHWH himself designates the precise standing-point for revelation. צוּר (tsur) = "rock/cliff" — later a title for God (Deut 32:4).

**22** "And it will be, when my glory [kevodi] passes by [ba-avor], I will put you [ve-samtikha] in the cleft [be-niqrat] of the rock [ha-tsur], and I will cover over you [ve-sakkoti] with my palm [kappi] until I have passed by [ad-ovri]."

> **Key terms:** נִקְרַת (niqrat) = "cleft/hole" — from נקר ("to bore/hollow out"). כַּפִּי (kappi) = "my palm/hand" — YHWH's hand as shelter and shield. וְשַׂכֹּתִי (ve-sakkoti) = "and I will cover/screen" — from סכך, the same root as סֻכָּה (sukkah, "booth"). **LXX:** σκεπάσω τῇ χειρί μου ("I will cover with my hand").

**23** "And I will remove [va-hasiroti] my palm [et-kappi], and you shall see [ve-raita] my back [et-akhorai]; but my face [u-fanai] shall not be seen [lo yera'u]."

> **Key terms:** אֲחֹרָי (akhorai) = "my back/what-is-behind-me" — from אָחוֹר (akhor, "back, behind"); YHWH can only be known in retrospect, after he has passed. וּפָנַי לֹא יֵרָאוּ (u-fanai lo yera'u) = "and my face shall not be seen" — Niphal; the final word of the chapter seals the paradox: face-to-face speech (v. 11) coexists with the impossibility of seeing the face. **LXX:** τὰ ὀπίσω μου ("my back-parts") and τὸ πρόσωπόν μου οὐκ ὀφθήσεταί σοι ("my face shall not be seen by you").

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **The Face Paradox (v. 11 vs. v. 20, 23):** MT states YHWH spoke with Moses פָּנִים אֶל־פָּנִים ("face to face," v. 11), yet declares לֹא־יִרְאַנִי הָאָדָם וָחָי ("no man shall see me and live," v. 20), and concludes וּפָנַי לֹא יֵרָאוּ ("my face shall not be seen," v. 23). The Hebrew text holds these in unresolved tension. The Rabbis distinguished "speaking face to face" (relational directness) from "seeing the face" (the unveiled fullness of divine glory). The LXX preserves this same tension without harmonization.

2. **The Tent Outside the Camp (v. 7):** The Ohel Mo'ed here is not the tabernacle of chs. 25–31 (not yet built). It is Moses' own tent, re-designated as a meeting place. Its placement outside the camp embodies YHWH's withdrawal from Israel's midst. The LXX's rendering as ἡ σκηνὴ τοῦ μαρτυρίου ("tent of testimony") assimilates it to the later tabernacle terminology.

3. **Stripping Ornaments from Horeb (v. 6):** The phrase מֵהַר חֹרֵב ("from Mount Horeb") is ambiguous — it may mean the stripping occurred at Horeb, or that it continued from Horeb onward. Most ancient interpreters took it as a permanent state of penitent austerity beginning at Sinai/Horeb.

4. **The Idem-per-idem Formula (v. 19):** The construction וְחַנֹּתִי אֶת־אֲשֶׁר אָחֹן ("I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious") is a classic Hebrew idiom expressing unqualified divine sovereignty. The same construction appears in Exodus 3:14 (אֶהְיֶה אֲשֶׁר אֶהְיֶה). Paul quotes the LXX of this verse in Romans 9:15.

5. **Goodness as Glory (v. 19):** When Moses asks to see YHWH's כָּבוֹד (kavod, "glory"), YHWH responds by promising to pass כָּל־טוּבִי (kol-tuvi, "all my goodness") before him. This is a deliberate theological redefinition: YHWH's glory is not raw power but moral goodness, which is then expounded in the name-proclamation of 34:6–7.

6. **The Cleft and the Palm (vv. 22–23):** The imagery of YHWH placing Moses in the נִקְרַת הַצּוּר ("cleft of the rock") and covering him with כַּפִּי ("my palm") is intensely anthropomorphic. The Targumim soften this; Onkelos renders כַּפִּי as מֵימְרִי ("my Word/Memra"). The LXX retains the anthropomorphism with τῇ χειρί μου ("my hand").

7. **LXX Expansion at v. 13:** Vaticanus adds ἐμφάνισόν μοι σεαυτόν ("reveal yourself to me"), a phrase absent from MT. This expansion heightens Moses' request and may reflect an early interpretive tradition linking "knowing the ways" to theophanic self-disclosure.

8. **Masoretic Division:** The Leningrad Codex shows a setumah (closed paragraph break) after v. 6, marking the transition from the command to depart to the tent-of-meeting narrative. Another break appears after v. 11, separating the tent narrative from Moses' direct petition.
